specs.reason: PRIMARY backing spec for issue #653 (gc module) and infrastructure spec for issue #666 (tracemalloc). Pre-clarification Q1 is entirely about gc.rs: atomic RC + cycle-detecting GC, mark-sweep with safepoint protocol, threshold at 700 allocs, gc.collect() triggering full mark-sweep, gc.disable()/enable() toggling automatic trigger. runtime/gc.md directly specifies: R2 (Mark-Sweep Collection Algorithm — mark phase, trace phase, sweep phase, reset phase matching Q1 exactly), R3 (Cycle Detection and Reclamation — foundation for GC correctness), R4 (__del__ Finalizer Invocation with resurrection semantics — gc module must expose finalizer behavior), R5 (Configurable Collection Thresholds — threshold_allocs=700 matching Q1, manual gc.collect() trigger, gc.disable()/enable() toggling per Q1), R1 (Track Container Objects — global container list backing tracemalloc get_traced_memory() coarse stats per Q3). All five requirements (R1-R5) are MANDATORY for implementing gc module.

PRIMARY backing spec for issue #655 (importlib). Pre-clarification Q2 is entirely about module.rs: thread-local MODULES HashMap, MbModule with name/file/attrs/is_package, mb_import() with circular import protection (sentinel pre-caching), mb_import_from() for selective imports, find_module() for search path. runtime/module.md directly specifies: R1 (Module path resolution — check current dir, sys.path, dotted names per Q2 find_module()), R2 (Module caching via sys.modules — insert before executing body; importlib.import_module() delegates here per Q2 exactly), R3 (Circular import handling — sentinel pre-caching matches Q2 mb_import() circular protection answer exactly), R4 (import/from-import syntax support — mb_import_from() backing importlib selective import per Q2), R5 (Module object attributes — __name__, __file__, __dict__, __package__ matching MbModule fields in Q2 answer). All five requirements (R1-R5) are MANDATORY for implementing importlib module.

PRIMARY backing spec for issue #654 (types module). Pre-clarification Q4 explicitly states: type() builtin works at builtins.rs:475 returning Instance with __name__ field; FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType etc. are NOT exposed as runtime type objects and need to be synthesized as Mamba class objects. runtime/builtins.md directly specifies: R5 (Enhanced P1 Builtins — 20 additional builtin functions with type() at builtins.rs:475 per Q4, entry point for runtime type-object synthesis of FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType), R6 (Symbol Registration — new FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType constants must be registered as mb_* symbols in symbols.rs per Q4's requirement to 'synthesize as Mamba class objects'), R3 (Type Checking Builtins — isinstance, issubclass enabling types.FunctionType isinstance(fn, types.FunctionType) checks per Q4 answer). All three requirements (R3, R5, R6) are MANDATORY for implementing types module.

IMPLEMENTATION-PATTERN spec for ALL 7 in-scope issues (#652 atexit, #653 gc, #654 types, #655 importlib, #656 codecs, #657 errno, #666 tracemalloc). Every issue requires creating a new native stdlib module file under runtime/stdlib/*.rs and registering it. stdlib/native-implementations.md defines: R2 (Module Rewrite — new *_mod.rs files must follow the pattern; atexit_mod.rs, gc_mod.rs, types_mod.rs, importlib_mod.rs, codecs_mod.rs, errno_mod.rs, tracemalloc_mod.rs all use this structure), R3 (Refined Symbol Registration — all new module symbols must be registered via register_symbols() in symbols.rs per Q2 mb_import delegation and Q4 type object synthesis), R4 (Error Mapping — Rust errors must map to MbException: codec errors (#656), errno libc errors (#657), and ModuleNotFoundError (#655) map to appropriate MbException types). The registration flow sequenceDiagram (runtime → stdlib_mod → module_registry → symbols_rs → JIT module) and error mapping flowchart apply to every new module. All three requirements (R2-R4) are MANDATORY for all 7 modules.

Symbol registration protocol spec required for all 7 new stdlib modules. Every new native stdlib module adds mb_* symbol registrations to symbols.rs. symbols.md R3 (register_symbols() Function) defines the exact registration protocol — cast function pointer to *const u8, register (name, address) pair with JIT module. R2 (MirExtern Declarations) defines the param_count/has_return fields required for each new symbol (e.g., mb_gc_collect, mb_gc_disable, mb_gc_enable for gc module). R4 (Naming Convention — mb_<category>_<operation> pattern) specifies that new gc module symbols must follow mb_gc_collect, mb_gc_disable, mb_gc_enable, and all stdlib module symbols must follow mb_<module>_<operation> pattern. This is a direct implementation requirement for all 7 issues, implementing the 'register_symbols() macro-driven registry' referenced in native-implementations.md R3. Three requirements (R2-R4) are MANDATORY for symbol registration.

Architectural background context only — provides high-level feature landscape, not direct issue implementation specs. R1 (Module System & Imports — lines 20-23) addresses import aliases, relative imports, multi-file compilation architecture as BACKGROUND for #655 importlib requirements (these address architectural foundation but not the mb_import/circular protection/find_module mechanics). R5 (Builtins Conformance — lines 40-42) addresses CPython conformance testing and dynamic type object synthesis as BACKGROUND for #654 type creation (matches Q4's 'synthesize Mamba class objects' goal but provides test strategy, not implementation mechanics). Low relevance because these are contextual references to architectural decisions, not direct implementation dependencies. Value is strategic alignment only.

Codebase navigation index only — provides directory structure revealing which runtime specs exist and where stdlib modules are implemented, but contains NO R-prefixed requirements or behavioral contracts. Lines 73, 87, 76 reference runtime/gc.md, runtime/module.md, and runtime/builtins.md respectively (all now directly included in reference context at HIGH relevance). Lines 91-127 show stdlib module directory structure indicating where new atexit_mod.rs, gc_mod.rs, types_mod.rs, importlib_mod.rs, codecs_mod.rs, errno_mod.rs, tracemalloc_mod.rs implementations will be located. Value is NAVIGATION ONLY for locating the actual implementation specs; no direct implementation dependency. Listed at LOW for completeness as documentation index.

notes: REVISION SUMMARY: Addressed all seven reviewer issues from initial reference context FAIL verdict. (1) Added runtime/gc.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #653 (gc module) and infrastructure for #666 (tracemalloc). Pre-clarification Q1 is entirely about gc.rs; spec covers R1-R5 including Mark-Sweep Algorithm (R2), Cycle Detection (R3), Finalizer semantics (R4), Configurable Thresholds at 700 allocs (R5), Container Tracking for tracemalloc (R1). All mandatory for gc module. (2) Added runtime/module.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #655 (importlib). Pre-clarification Q2 is entirely about module.rs; spec covers R1-R5 including Module path resolution (R1), Module caching in sys.modules (R2), Circular import handling with sentinel pre-caching (R3), import/from-import syntax (R4), Module object attributes (R5). All mandatory for importlib. (3) Added runtime/builtins.md (HIGH) — PRIMARY backing spec for issue #654 (types module). Pre-clarification Q4 explicitly cites builtins.rs:475 type() builtin; spec covers R3 (Type Checking Builtins), R5 (Enhanced P1 Builtins with type() entry point for type-object synthesis), R6 (Symbol Registration for FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType constants). All mandatory for types module. (4) Added stdlib/native-implementations.md (HIGH) — implementation-pattern spec for ALL 7 issues. Every new module (#652 atexit, #653 gc, #654 types, #655 importlib, #656 codecs, #657 errno, #666 tracemalloc) requires new *_mod.rs files following this pattern. Spec covers R2 (Module Rewrite), R3 (Symbol Registration via register_symbols()), R4 (Error Mapping to MbException). All mandatory for all 7 modules. (5) Added runtime/symbols.md (MEDIUM) — Symbol registration protocol spec. Defines register_symbols() protocol (R3), MirExtern Declarations (R2), and Naming Convention pattern mb_<category>_<operation> (R4) required for all 7 new module symbols. Medium relevance because architecturally prescriptive but implements the registration pattern from native-implementations.md R3. (6) Downgraded all-mamba-p0.md to LOW — provides architectural background (R1 Module System architecture for #655, R5 Builtins Conformance for #654) but NOT direct implementation specs. High-level feature landscape, not mechanics. (7) Downgraded README.md to LOW — pure codebase index with no R-prefixed requirements. Value is navigation only (points to gc.md, module.md, builtins.md in specs; shows stdlib module directory). (8) REMOVED pattern-matching.md — PEP 634 match/case implementation has ZERO connection to stdlib-system issues. No mention of atexit, gc, types, importlib, codecs, errno, or tracemalloc in R1-R8. Belongs to stdlib-introspection group. (9) BLOCKED: Cannot include stdlib/atexit.md, stdlib/codecs.md, stdlib/errno.md because they do not exist yet. These are NEW DELIVERABLES for the change-spec phase. Reference context lists EXISTING specs only. FINAL COVERAGE: All 7 issues (#652-#657, #666) now have direct backing specs. All pre-clarification questions Q1-Q4 have comprehensive spec coverage with explicit requirement mappings. Symbol registration protocol specified for all 7 modules.
